channel.title: Zonted
channel.description: I test AI tools so you don't have to. Honest reviews, real data, and opinions from someone who actually builds with this stuff.

item.title: OpenClaw vs Claude Code: I Choose Freedom
item.description: A 5-PR run on tabiji content cost $137 in API tokens last week. The same throughput on a Max plan is a ~9× subsidy. Subsidies end. Why my recurring crons live on OpenClaw with 21 models across 5 providers, and Claude Code stays for one-offs.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Content Traffic is Vanity. Training Data is the Moat.
item.description: Our forgotten API is 83% Meta crawler. Flattering, wrong metric. I uploaded tabiji
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Scaling with AI is Hard because AI is Lazy
item.description: Fifty cleanup PRs in five weeks. Fake restaurants, fake subreddits, 4,270 null-island coordinates, 5,096 fabricated Reddit quotes. Why AI generates plausible-but-wrong content at scale, and the railguard stack that finally works.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: AI Comics: Do's & Don'ts, 5+ Models Tested
item.description: We tested Midjourney, Seedream v5 Lite, Wan 2.7 Pro, Qwen Image 2.0, and Nano Banana Pro — then shipped 733 comics across eight countries. Full prompts, scripts, costs, and the postmortem on what silently broke at multi-country scale.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The Future of Software is Headless
item.description: Salesforce is going headless. Agents, not humans, are now the primary consumer of software. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: AEO Is Not SEO — Here's the Playbook
item.description: Traffic is declining and it
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Rise of AI Influencers & Fall of Trust on the Internet — Fake Until Proven Real
item.description: A fake AI monk has 2.6 million followers and made $300K in 90 days. 76% of the people consuming AI content are over 45. The internet is becoming synthetic faster than we can label it.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Best AI Video Models in 2026: I Tested 6 Text-to-Video APIs for Travel Reels
item.description: I tested Wan 2.7, Kling 3.0, Kling O3, Seedance 2.0, Veo 3.1 Lite, and Grok Imagine on the same Cancun travel-reel prompt, then reran the two winners on a Puerto Morelos follow-up shot. Here are the exact prompts, outputs, and my split verdict.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Human-in-the-Loop: How AI Orchestrator Became My Full-Time Job
item.description: I have 20 AI agents running 24/7. The fantasy was that they
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Wavespeed Review: The Best AI Video API
item.description: Hands-on review of Wavespeed — the AI inference platform with 1,000+ models, pay-per-use pricing, and a unified API. After 200+ reels through their pipeline, here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: AI Psychosis
item.description: AI tools don
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: MakeUGC Review: AI UGC Ads Worth $10 a Clip?
item.description: Hands-on review of MakeUGC — the AI UGC ad platform with 300+ avatars, Seedance 2.0, and Veo3 access. We break down pricing, output quality, and whether it
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The Day Claude Banned OpenClaw
item.description: OpenClaw banned Claude and Anthropic on Saturday. The community scrambled. We tested GPT 5.4, GLM 5.1, MiniMax M2.7, MIMO, and Qwen 3.6 — and landed on a hybrid setup that actually works better than before.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: What Is AI Reward Hacking?
item.description: AI reward hacking is when your AI agent finds shortcuts to hit your goals while quietly destroying quality. We lost hundreds of pages to thin, non-factual content. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: What Is AI Self-Healing?
item.description: AI self-healing is when an AI agent detects, diagnoses, and fixes failures autonomously. We walk through a real incident where our AI agent fixed a production failure at 4 AM — no human involved.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: When Token Costs Hit Zero — The Local Model Revolution Is Already Here
item.description: Frontier models spend billions on data centers. But better hardware and local models like Qwen 3.5 are making token costs irrelevant. The future of AI runs on your laptop.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: AI Resilience Planning
item.description: Claude API was down 14 hours last quarter. We benchmarked MiniMax M2.7 vs Claude Opus 4.6 on identical tasks — here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Stop Optimizing Your AI Stack
item.description: Token optimization, memory persistence, context window hacks — most AI infrastructure work is a trap. Models are improving faster than your optimizations matter. Build the thing instead.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The Great API Shutdown
item.description: The open API era that fueled a decade of internet innovation is ending. Platforms are locking down data access to fight AI training, monetize developers, and consolidate control. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: OpenClaw Is an MMORPG
item.description: A fresh OpenClaw install is a level 0 character — full of potential, zero abilities. Every API key you add, every skill you install is a talent point. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: What Is AI Drift?
item.description: AI drift is what happens when AI-generated content gradually deviates from your template, tone, and quality standard at scale. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The Future Is Synthetic — AI-Generated Content, Music, and Personalized Experiences
item.description: We run 4 AI agents that produce music, Instagram Reels, travel content, and more — all day, every day. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The Economics of the Internet Are Broken
item.description: AI agents killed the publisher incentive model the same way streaming killed the 99-cent MP3. When producing a video costs $0.30 and promoting it costs $50, the math breaks. Here
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The Future of Content Is Data Enrichment
item.description: AI agents don
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: The True Cost of AI Content Production
item.description: Everyone obsesses over model costs and token prices. But after producing 400+ pages and 200+ videos with AI, we learned the real expense is data enrichment — Google Places API, SerpAPI, Reddit scraping, and the infrastructure to make AI output actually useful.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Why AI Slop Is Necessary
item.description: The goal isn
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Nano Banana 2 vs Grok for Concept Art
item.description: We tested Nano Banana 2 (Gemini 3.1 Flash) against Grok (xAI) on 8 identical lofi anime art prompts. NB2 scored 9.0/10 vs Grok
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: Suno vs MiniMax Music: Which AI Composer Wins?
item.description: We tested Suno AI and MiniMax Music 2.0/2.5/2.5+ in production across 200+ Instagram Reels. Reverse-engineered the Suno API, scored piano covers with Gemini, and found the best AI music workflow. Real audio samples, costs, and code.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: 5 AI Video Generators, 50 Reels: What Actually Works
item.description: We tested 5 AI video models across 50+ Instagram Reels in production. The biggest lesson: text-to-video is not ready — image-to-video is the only path. Real costs, real data, real opinions.
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: AI Image Generation: 5 Models, 26 Real Results
item.description: We tested GPT, Grok, Gemini (Nano Banana 2), MiniMax, and CogView-4 across 26 images in two real production workflows. The biggest differentiator wasn
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)

item.title: AI Reels: What Actually Works
item.description: We tested 100% AI-generated Instagram Reels, YouTube Shorts, and X posts using Veo 3, Nano Banana 2, and FFmpeg. Real view counts across all 3 platforms — what works, what doesn
item.author: bernard@zonted.com (Bernard Huang)
